content:
Hello everyone, I'm quite confused about how to rewrite this part of the code in Rust. I can now call the open methods in my module, but when I try to implement it in a similar way to the C language, I encounter a "sh: write error: Input/output error".
C module part：

static struct tty_port *tport;
static struct tty0tty_serial **tty0tty_table;   /* initially all NULL */


static int tty0tty_open(struct tty_struct *tty, struct file *file)
{
    struct tty0tty_serial *tty0tty;
    int index;
    int mcr = 0;


    /* initialize the pointer in case something fails */
    tty->driver_data = NULL;

    /* get the serial object associated with this tty pointer */
    index = tty->index;
    tty0tty = tty0tty_table[index];
    ..................................

    tport[index].tty = tty;
    tty->port = &tport[index];

    if ((index % 2) == 0) {
        if (tty0tty_table[index + 1] != NULL)
            if (tty0tty_table[index + 1]->open_count > 0)
                mcr = tty0tty_table[index + 1]->mcr;
    } else {
        if (tty0tty_table[index - 1] != NULL)
            if (tty0tty_table[index - 1]->open_count > 0)
                mcr = tty0tty_table[index - 1]->mcr;
    }

        ......................................

    return 0;
}

static int __init tty0tty_init(void)
{
    int retval;
    int i;
...............................................
    tport = kmalloc(2 * pairs * sizeof(struct tty_port), GFP_KERNEL);
    tty0tty_table =
        kmalloc(2 * pairs * sizeof(struct tty0tty_serial *), GFP_KERNEL);

    for (i = 0; i < 2 * pairs; i++) {
        tty0tty_table[i] = NULL;
    }
..................................
}

my rust module：

static mut TPORT: *mut bindings::tty_port = core::ptr::null_mut();
static mut TTY0TTY_TABLE: *mut *mut Tty0ttySerial = ptr::null_mut();

extern "C" fn tty0tty_open(tty: *mut bindings::tty_struct, file: *mut kernel::bindings::file) -> i32 {
    let mut tty0tty: *mut Tty0ttySerial = core::ptr::null_mut();
    let index: i32;
    let mut mcr: i32 = 0;

    unsafe {
        (*tty).driver_data = core::ptr::null_mut();
        index = (*tty).index;
        tty0tty = *TTY0TTY_TABLE.offset(index as isize);//here meet error, null pointer deference
       .......................................

            tty0tty = Box::into_raw(tty0tty_box);
            *TTY0TTY_TABLE.offset(index as isize) = tty0tty;
        }

        (*TPORT.offset(index as isize)).tty = tty;
        (*tty).port = &mut (*TPORT.offset(index as isize));

        if index % 2 == 0 {
            pr_info!("enter index % 2 == 0");
            let table_entry = *TTY0TTY_TABLE.offset(index as isize + 1);
            pr_info!("assign table_entty");
            if !table_entry.is_null() {
                pr_info!("entry pass !null check");
                if (*table_entry).open_count > 0 {
                    pr_info!("open >0 write mcr");
                    (*table_entry).mcr = mcr;
                }
            }
            else{
                pr_info!("table entry is null");
            }
        } else {
            let table_entry = *TTY0TTY_TABLE.offset(index as isize - 1);
            if !table_entry.is_null() {
                if (*table_entry).open_count > 0 {
                    (*table_entry).mcr = mcr;
                }
            }
        }

       .........................................
}
    pr_info!("open success");
    0
}

impl kernel::Module for Tty0tty {
    fn init(_name: &'static CStr, mut _module: &'static ThisModule) -> Result<Self> {
    ..........................................

         // read pairs，cal pair_count
         let lock = _module.kernel_param_lock();
         let pair_count = 2 * pairs.read(&lock);
         let mut tport_vec: Vec<bindings::tty_port> = Vec::try_with_capacity((pair_count).try_into().unwrap()).unwrap();
        for _ in 0..(2 * pair_count) {
            tport_vec.try_push(unsafe { mem::zeroed() }).unwrap();
        }

        let mut table_vec: Vec<*mut Tty0ttySerial> = Vec::try_with_capacity((pair_count).try_into().unwrap()).unwrap();
        for _ in 0..pair_count {
            table_vec.try_push(ptr::null_mut()).unwrap();
        }
        unsafe {
            TPORT = Box::into_raw(tport_vec.try_into_boxed_slice().unwrap()) as *mut bindings::tty_port;
            TTY0TTY_TABLE = Box::into_raw(table_vec.try_into_boxed_slice().unwrap()) as *mut *mut Tty0ttySerial;
        }
}
}

vm log： 

echo "lwz" > /dev/tnt0
[   11.480805] tty_test: the tty0tty is null
[   11.480849] tty_test: enter index % 2 == 0
[   11.480921] tty_test: assign table_entty
[   11.480955] tty_test: table entry is null
sh: write error: Input/output error
[   11.481012] tty_test: open success
[   11.482175] tty_test: starting close
[   11.482249] tty_test: pass index check
/ #

i can figure out the proble maybe in " let table_entry = *TTY0TTY_TABLE.offset(index as isize + 1);" the log shows it is null, but i don't know how to slove it. Maybe i need to impl it from 'kmalloc' part :(
Does tty0tty_open get called before or after you alloc for TPORT and TPORT_TABLE?

I didn't call tty0tty_open in my module. when i load my rust module and run it in QEMU, i can find 'tnt0--tnt7' in /dev.

/ # ls /dev/tnt*
/dev/tnt0  /dev/tnt2  /dev/tnt4  /dev/tnt6
/dev/tnt1  /dev/tnt3  /dev/tnt5  /dev/tnt7

So i use "echo '....' > /dev/tnt0" to test it :

echo 'lwz' > /dev/tnt0
[   13.694191] tty_test: the tty0tty is null
[   13.694254] tty_test: enter index % 2 == 0
[   13.694305] tty_test: assign table_entty
[   13.694336] tty_test: table entry is null
^C[   13.694420] tty_test: open success
[   24.395161] tty_test: starting close
[   24.395234] tty_test: pass index check
/ #

vm log： 

echo "lwz" > /dev/tnt0
[   11.480805] tty_test: the tty0tty is null
[   11.480849] tty_test: enter index % 2 == 0

[   11.480955] tty_test: table entry is null
sh: write error: Input/output error

[   11.482175] tty_test: starting close
[   11.482249] tty_test: pass index check
/ #

this problem may because i didn't impl 'write' method at that time. after i impl 'write' method there is no "sh: write error: Input/output error" but still "[ 11.480955] tty_test: table entry is null"

also apply my current open, close, write and write_room method here:

extern "C" fn tty0tty_open(tty: *mut bindings::tty_struct, file: *mut kernel::bindings::file) -> i32 {
    let mut tty0tty: *mut Tty0ttySerial = core::ptr::null_mut();
    let index: i32;
    let mut msr: u32 = 0;
    let mut mcr: u32 = 0;

    unsafe {
        (*tty).driver_data = core::ptr::null_mut();
        index = (*tty).index;
        tty0tty = *TTY0TTY_TABLE.offset(index as isize);//here meet error, null pointer deference
        if tty0tty.is_null() {
            pr_info!("the tty0tty is null");
            let tty0tty_box = match Box::try_new(Tty0ttySerial::new()) {
                Ok(boxed) => boxed,
                Err(_) => {
                    pr_err!("Failed to allocate memory for Tty0ttySerial");
                    return -ENOMEM; // ENOMEM
                }
            };

            tty0tty = Box::into_raw(tty0tty_box);
            *TTY0TTY_TABLE.offset(index as isize) = tty0tty;
        }

        (*TPORT.offset(index as isize)).tty = tty;
        (*tty).port = &mut (*TPORT.offset(index as isize));

        if index % 2 == 0 {
            pr_info!("enter index % 2 == 0");
            let table_entry = *TTY0TTY_TABLE.offset(index as isize + 1);
            pr_info!("assign table_entty");
            if !table_entry.is_null() {
                pr_info!("entry pass !null check");
                if (*table_entry).open_count > 0 {
                    pr_info!("open >0 write mcr");
                    (*table_entry).mcr = mcr;
                }
            }
            /*else{pr_info!("table entry is null");}*/
        } else {
            let table_entry = *TTY0TTY_TABLE.offset(index as isize - 1);
            if !table_entry.is_null() {
                if (*table_entry).open_count > 0 {
                    (*table_entry).mcr = mcr;
                }
            }
        }

        if (mcr & MCR_RTS) == MCR_RTS {
            msr |= MSR_CTS;
        }

        if (mcr & MCR_DTR) == MCR_DTR {
            msr |= MSR_DSR | MSR_CD;
        }

        (*tty0tty).msr = msr;
        (*tty0tty).mcr = 0;

        use core::ffi::c_void;
        (*tty).driver_data = tty0tty as *mut c_void;
        (*tty0tty).tty = tty;

        (*tty0tty).open_count += 1;
}
    pr_info!("open success");
    0
}


fn do_close(tty0tty: *mut Tty0ttySerial) {
    pr_info!("starting close");
    unsafe {
        if tty0tty.is_null() || (*tty0tty).tty.is_null() {
            return;
        }

        let mut msr = 0;
        let index = (*(*tty0tty).tty).index;
        if index % 2 == 0 {
            let table_entry = *TTY0TTY_TABLE.offset(index as isize + 1);
            if !table_entry.is_null() {
                if (*table_entry).open_count > 0 {
                    (*table_entry).msr = msr;
                }
            }
        } else {
            let table_entry = *TTY0TTY_TABLE.offset(index as isize - 1);
            if !table_entry.is_null() {
                if (*table_entry).open_count > 0 {
                    (*table_entry).msr = msr;
                }
            }
        }
        pr_info!("pass index check");

        if (*tty0tty).open_count == 0{
            return;
        }
        else{
            (*tty0tty).open_count-=1;
        }
    }
}

extern "C" fn tty0tty_close(tty: *mut bindings::tty_struct, file: *mut kernel::bindings::file) {
    unsafe {
        if tty.is_null() || (*tty).driver_data.is_null() {
            pr_info!("tty is null");
            return; // or handle appropriately
        }

        let tty0tty = (*tty).driver_data as *mut Tty0ttySerial;
        do_close(tty0tty);
        pr_info!("close success");
    }
}

extern "C" fn tty0tty_write(tty: *mut bindings::tty_struct, buffer: *const u8, count: i32) -> i32{
    let mut retval=0;
    unsafe {
        let tty0tty = (*tty ).driver_data as *mut Tty0ttySerial;

        let mut ttyx: *mut bindings::tty_struct = core::ptr::null_mut();


        if tty0tty.is_null(){
            pr_info!("tty0tty write is null");
            return -ENOMEM;
        }

        if (*tty0tty).open_count==0{
            return retval;
        }

        let index = (*(*tty0tty).tty).index;
        if index % 2 == 0 {
            let table_entry = *TTY0TTY_TABLE.offset(index as isize + 1);
            if !table_entry.is_null() {
                if (*table_entry).open_count > 0 {
                    let ttyx = unsafe { (*table_entry).tty };
                }
            }
        } else {
            let table_entry = *TTY0TTY_TABLE.offset(index as isize - 1);
            if !table_entry.is_null() {
                if (*table_entry).open_count > 0 {
                    let ttyx = unsafe { (*table_entry).tty };
                }
            }
        }

        if !ttyx.is_null(){
            bindings::tty_insert_flip_string_fixed_flag((*ttyx).port, buffer, 0,count.try_into().unwrap());
            bindings::tty_flip_buffer_push((*ttyx).port);
            retval=count;
        }



    }

    retval
}

extern "C" fn tty0tty_write_room(tty: *mut bindings::tty_struct) -> u32{
    let mut room=0;
    unsafe {
        let tty0tty = (*tty ).driver_data as *mut Tty0ttySerial;
        if tty0tty.is_null(){
            return 1;//here is some problem
        }

        if (*tty0tty).open_count==0{
            return room;
        }
    }
    room=255;
    room
}
